Alomomola

#0593 Jellicent #0595 Joltik
  • Contents
  • Info
  • Base stats
  • Evolution chart
  • Pokédex entries
  • Moves learned
  • Sprites
  • Locations
  • Language

Alomomola is a Water type Pokémon introduced in Generation 5.

Alomomola

Additional artwork

Pokédex data

National № 0594
Type Water
Species Caring Pokémon
Height 1.2 m (3′11″)
Weight 31.6 kg (69.7 lbs)
Abilities 1. Healer
2. Hydration
Regenerator (hidden ability)
Local № 0100 (Black/White)
0182 (Black 2/White 2)
0149 (X/Y — Coastal Kalos)
0157 (Sun/Moon — Alola dex)
0191 (U.Sun/U.Moon — Alola dex)
0336 (Scarlet/Violet)
0085 (The Indigo Disk)

Training

EV yield 2 HP
Catch rate 75 (9.8% with PokéBall, full HP)
Base Friendship 50 (normal)
Base Exp. 165
Growth Rate Fast

Breeding

Egg Groups Water 1, Water 2
Gender 50% male, 50% female
Egg cycles 40 (10,024–10,280 steps)

Base stats

HP 165
440 534
Attack 75
139 273
Defense 80
148 284
Sp. Atk 40
76 196
Sp. Def 45
85 207
Speed 65
121 251
Total 470 Min Max

The ranges shown on the right are for a level 100 Pokémon. Maximum values are based on a beneficial nature, 252 EVs, 31 IVs; minimum values are based on a hindering nature, 0 EVs, 0 IVs.

Type defenses

The effectiveness of each type on Alomomola.

NorFirWatEleGraIceFigPoiGro
½½22½
FlyPsyBugRocGhoDraDarSteFai
½

Evolution chart

  • Alomomola does not evolve.

Alomomola changes

  • In Generations 5-7, Alomomola has a base Friendship value of 70.

Pokédex entries

Black The special membrane enveloping Alomomola has the ability to heal wounds.
White Floating in the open sea is how they live. When they find a wounded Pokémon, they embrace it and bring it to shore.
Black 2
White 2		 It gently holds injured and weak Pokémon in its fins. Its special membrane heals their wounds.
X
Omega Ruby		 Floating in the open sea is how they live. When they find a wounded Pokémon, they embrace it and bring it to shore.
Y
Alpha Sapphire		 It gently holds injured and weak Pokémon in its fins. Its special membrane heals their wounds.
Sun It uses its special mucus to close the wounds of injured Pokémon. The reason for this behavior remains unknown.
Moon They float upon the open sea. Many water Pokémon gather in the area around Alomomola.
Ultra Sun Fishermen take them along on long voyages, because if you have an Alomomola with you, there’ll be no need for a doctor or medicine.
Ultra Moon The reason it helps Pokémon in a weakened condition is that any Pokémon coming after them may also attack Alomomola.
Scarlet It gently holds injured and weak Pokémon in its fins. Its special membrane heals their wounds.
Violet They float upon the open sea. Many water Pokémon gather in the area around Alomomola.

Moves learned by Alomomola

  • In other generations
  • 5
  • 6
  • 7
  • 8
  • 9
Legends: Z-A Scarlet/Violet

Moves learnt by level up

Alomomola does not learn any level up moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Moves learnt by TM

Alomomola cannot be taught any TM moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

German Mamolida

Italian Alomomola

Name origin

mola mola
a species of fish
